awards.title: Leaf Disease Detection
descripList: Built a leaf disease detection project as part of GDSC, using machine learning to identify plant diseases from leaf images.

awards.title: Abacus — Large-Scale Data Preprocessing
descripList: Engineered a high-throughput data preprocessing pipeline handling massive, messy datasets — cleaning, normalizing, and transforming raw inputs into analysis-ready features at scale.
Optimized for speed and reliability, turning hours of manual wrangling into a streamlined, reproducible workflow.

awards.title: LeetCode Knight
descripList: Earned the prestigious Knight badge by ranking in the top 5% of LeetCode contest participants worldwide.
Consistent performance across weekly and biweekly contests, solving complex algorithmic problems under time pressure.

awards.title: Codeforces Competitive Programmer
descripList: Active competitive programmer on Codeforces, regularly participating in rated rounds.
Sharpened algorithmic thinking and problem-solving speed through consistent contest participation.

awards.title: CodeChef 3-Star (Max Rating 1736)
descripList: Achieved a 3-star rating on CodeChef with a peak rating of 1736 (Div 2).
Consistent participation in Long Challenges, Cook-Offs, and Dinnertime contests.
A deep learning model that detects and segments lung nodules from CT scan images using a U-Net based architecture.
Deployed as an interactive web app on Hugging Face Spaces — upload a CT scan and visualize ground truth vs. predicted segmentation masks side by side.
A deep learning model that detects diseases in potato plants from leaf images using a Convolutional Neural Network.
Deployed as an interactive Streamlit web app — upload a leaf image and get instant disease predictions.
A front-end clone of the Amazon shopping website built with vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
A multi-role Spring Boot backend (Patient, Doctor, Lab Technician, Admin) with 40+ REST APIs, Role-Based Access Control via Spring Security, and a 3-layered architecture.
Deployed live on Render with an interactive Swagger UI demo (no setup needed). Use credentials tushar / 1234 to try out the secured endpoints.
Note: free tier sleeps after 15 min idle — first request may take ~30s to wake up.
An website that uses the openWeather api to fetch data about the current climate changes and display it on the website.
